steps: Set $y = f(x) + 6 = 0$ to find the x-intercept: $f(x) = -6$.
Solve for $x$: $26x = \dfrac{-8}{d} - 27 = \dfrac{-8-27d}{d} \Rightarrow x = \dfrac{-8-27d}{26d}$. Multiply numerator and denominator by 3: $x = \dfrac{-24-81d}{78d} = \dfrac{-81d-24}{78d}$.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$\left(\frac{-81d+24}{78d},0\right)$': The numerator has $+24$ but our derivation gives $-24$. Sign error. ✗
  • Choice B — '$\left(\frac{-81d-24}{78d},0\right)$': Numerator $-81d-24$ and denominator $78d$ match exactly what was derived. ✓
  • Choice C — '$\left(6-\frac{81d+18}{78d},0\right)$': This incorrectly adds 6 to the x-coordinate; the $+6$ shifts the graph vertically, not horizontally. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\left(-\frac{45}{78d+6},0\right)$': The denominator $78d+6$ does not arise from correct algebra; the 6 should have been absorbed in the numerator, not the denominator. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
